query: What have been the daily maximum PLA aircraft incursions into Taiwan ADIZ reported by Taiwan MND in each month of 2025 and 2026 so far? List the single-day record for each month, and any large exercises (Joint Sword, Strait Thunder) with their peak single-day aircraft counts.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Findings

**No source publishes a clean month-by-month daily-maximum table** for 2025–2026. Taiwan MND figures also mix two metrics (total aircraft detected in 24h vs. those that entered the ADIZ/crossed the median line), so headline "records" must be read carefully. The best-documented single-day peaks cluster around major exercises:

### 2025 — a record year, peaking at year-end
- **Total 2025 ADIZ sorties: ~3,764** (+22.4% vs 2024)
- **Strait Thunder-2025A** (Apr 1–2): 135 aircraft over 2 days, 68 into ADIZ
- **August 2025 surge** (~Aug 8–9): >80 aircraft over Fri–Sat — biggest since Joint Sword-2024B
- **Justice Mission-2025** (Dec 29–30): **Dec 29 peaked at 130 aircraft with 90 median-line/ADIZ crossings** — the highest single-day total of 2025 (Dec 30: 71 total, 35 crossings)
- Still **below the all-time record of 153 total (111 ADIZ) on Oct 14, 2024** (Joint Sword-2024B)

### 2026 — a dramatic, historically rare collapse
- **Zero PLA aircraft in the ADIZ from Feb 27 to at least Mar 5, 2026** (unprecedented multi-day lull)
- Only **460 aircraft YTD by Mar 5 — down 46.5% year-over-year**
- **2026 daily average ~4.5 aircraft**; pre-lull totals were modest (Jan 29=19, Feb 12=32, Feb 26=22)
- **March 14 spike:** 26 detected, 16 into ADIZ — only the 8th-largest of 2026
- Flights **resumed mid-March 2026**, reportedly after a Trump–Xi meeting delay

The full report, structured data points, and sources have been recorded. The main limitation: precise per-month maxima for non-exercise months would require pulling the raw MND / CSIS ChinaPower / PLATracker daily dataset directly.

key_facts: 2025 total PLA ADIZ incursions: ~3,764 aircraft sorties, a record and +22.4% vs 2024 (CSIS ChinaPower, Feb 5 2026).
All-time single-day record remains 153 total aircraft (111 entered ADIZ) on Oct 14, 2024 during Joint Sword-2024B; not exceeded in 2025 or 2026.
Highest single-day total of 2025: Dec 29, 2025 during Justice Mission-2025 = 130 aircraft detected, with 90 crossing the median line/entering the ADIZ.
Justice Mission-2025 Day 2 (Dec 30): 71 aircraft total, 35 crossing the median line.
Strait Thunder-2025A (Apr 1-2, 2025): 135 aircraft sorties over two days, 68 entered the ADIZ.
August 2025 surge (~Aug 8-9): more than 80 aircraft over Friday-Saturday, described as the biggest incursion since Joint Sword-2024B (Oct 2024).
Early 2026 saw a historically rare collapse: MND reported ZERO PLA aircraft in the ADIZ/median-line area from Feb 27 to at least Mar 5, 2026.
By Mar 5, 2026 only 460 aircraft had entered the ADIZ year-to-date, a 46.5% drop versus the same period in 2025 (Reuters/Secure Taiwan Association).
2026 daily average fell to ~4.5 aircraft (Taiwan Security Monitor).
Pre-lull 2026 daily totals were modest: Jan 29 = 19, Feb 12 = 32, Feb 26 = 22 aircraft.
March 14, 2026 brief spike: 26 aircraft detected, 16 entered the ADIZ — only the 8th-largest ADIZ incursion of 2026.
Chinese flights resumed mid-March 2026 after a reported Trump-Xi meeting delay (Washington Times, Mar 16 2026).
No public source provides a consolidated month-by-month daily-maximum table for 2025-2026; MND mixes 'total detected in 24h' vs 'entered ADIZ/crossed median line'.
After May 2024, monthly ADIZ sorties never dropped below ~209; May 2024-Dec 2025 monthly average was ~319 (CSIS ChinaPower).
